sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) flesh, i.q. בָּשָׂר, but mostly poet. (As to its origin I say nothing [in Thes. from the idea of. turgidity]), Psa 73:26 78:20 , 27 Psa 78:27. Jer 51:35, חֲמָכִי וּשְׁאֵרִי עַל־בָּבֶל “my outrage and my flesh (sc. devoured by the Chaldeans, as if my blood shed by the Chaldeans) come upon Babylon” (compare אָבַל No. 1, letter g). Meton.-

(2) a relation by blood, relations by blood, i.q. בָּשָׁר No. 3, Lev 18:6, 12 Lev 18:12, 13 Lev 18:13fully שְׁאֵר בְּשָׂרֹו Lev 25:49.

(3) any food, or aliment, Exo 21:10.
